concerns.text: T014 restricts to 'JSON edge-list format only' but FR-001 explicitly allows 'edge list or JSON'. This is a silent constraint weakening without spec amendment.
T017 implements 'pre-cascade network context' but FR-002 requires 'cascade depth' as a network-level feature. The spec requirement is not preserved in tasks.
T031 implements 'leave-one-user-out cross-validation' but FR-005 requires 'k-fold cross-validation'. This is a constraint weakening without spec amendment.
T036 verifies '3 predictors have posterior prob > 0.95' but SC-001 also requires 'consistent effect direction across folds'. This acceptance criterion is dropped from the task.
T010 validates '2,000 node limit' but spec Edge Cases state '10,000 nodes'. The plan reduced this without spec amendment, and tasks reflect the plan change not the spec.
Phase 6 (T045-T053) adds content features scope expansion beyond spec. No spec amendment authorizes this addition; tasks introduce requirements not in spec.md.
Constitution Principle I requires 'Random seeds MUST be pinned in code/'. Tasks mention seeds in run_pipeline.sh (T024) but no task explicitly pins seeds in code/ scripts.
Constitution Principle III requires 'Datasets MUST be checksummed'. T028 generates manifest.json with data hashes but no task explicitly checksums input datasets under data/.
Constitution Principle VI requires model_spec.yaml to be 'referenced in the manuscript'. T054 mentions documentation updates but no task explicitly references model_spec.yaml in manuscript.
Constitution Principle VII requires feature engineering scripts to 'record input data sources, transformation steps, and output files'. Tasks implement scripts but don't require this documentation.
